title: Benzoyl-m-nitroacetanilide as a Gravimetric Reagent for Vanadium(IV)
author: SANAT KUMAR MANDAL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, The University of Burdwan, Burdwan, 713101 

  Manuscript received 21 March 1975; revised 30 July 1975: accepted 21 August 1975  

 Benzoyl- m -nitroacetanilide has been used to determine 6.0 to 14.0 mg of vanadium(IV) gravi­metrically with a relative standard deviation of 10.17% for 30 determinations. The green complex VO(C 15 H 11 O 4 N 2 ) 2  is precipitated in the  p h-range 4.0 to 5.5 adjusted with ammonium acetate (5%). and is weighed after drying it at 110°. The presence of Ag + , TI + , F', I' or sulphosalicylic acid do not interfere in the determination. Vanadium has been separated from Ni 2+ , Co 2+ , Mn 2+ , Zn 2+ , Al 3+ , Cr 3+ , Fe 3+ , Ti 4+ ,  \(MoO_4^{2-}\) ,  \(WO_4^{2-}\) or  \(UO_2^{2+}\)  using sulphosalicylic acid as the masking agent, while, iodide has been used to mask Hg 2 + and Cd 2+ . The method is applicable for the determi­nation of the vanadium content of chrome-vanadium steel.
